# i18n

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `i18n`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `i18n.defaultCharset` | `string` | The default locale character set. |
| `i18n.defaultLocale` | `string` | The default locale. It determines the language for program messages, the format for dates and times, sort order, and so on. Setting the default character set is done via {option}`i18n.defaultCharset`. |
| `i18n.extraLocaleSettings` | `attribute set of string` | A set of additional system-wide locale settings other than `LANG` which can be configured with {option}`i18n.defaultLocale`. Note that the `/UTF-8` suffix used in {option}`i18n.extraLocales` indicates a character set, and it must not be added manually here. To use a non-`UTF-8` character set such as ISO-XXXX-8, the {option}`i18n.localeCharsets` can be used. Note that if the [`LANGUAGE` key](https://www.gnu.org/software/gettext/manual/html_node/The-LANGUAGE-variable.html) is used in this option, it is ignored when computing the locales required to be installed, because the possible values of this key are more diverse and flexible then the others. |
| `i18n.extraLocales` | `(list of string) or value "all" (singular enum)` | Additional locales that the system should support, besides the ones configured with {option}`i18n.defaultLocale` and {option}`i18n.extraLocaleSettings`. Set this to `"all"` to install all available locales. |
| `i18n.glibcLocales` | `null or absolute path` | Customized pkg.glibcLocales package. Changing this option can disable handling of i18n.defaultLocale and supportedLocale. |
| `i18n.imperativeLocale` | `boolean` | Whether to enable imperative locale and keyboard management via localectl. When enabled, locale and keyboard settings can be changed at runtime using `localectl set-locale` and `localectl set-keymap`. When disabled (the default), these settings are managed declaratively through {option}`i18n.defaultLocale`, {option}`i18n.extraLocaleSettings`, and {option}`console.keyMap`. . |
| `i18n.inputMethod.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable an additional input method type. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.enableGtk2` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Gtk2 support. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.enableGtk3` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Gtk3 support. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.enabled` | `null or one of "ibus", "fcitx5", "nabi", "uim", "hime", "kime"` | Deprecated - use `type` and `enable = true` instead |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.addons` | `list of package` | Enabled Fcitx5 addons. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.ignoreUserConfig` | `boolean` | Ignore the user configures. **Warning**: When this is enabled, the user config files are totally ignored and the user dict can't be saved and loaded. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.quickPhrase` | `attribute set of string` | Quick phrases. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.quickPhraseFiles` | `attribute set of absolute path` | Quick phrase files. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.settings.addons` | `attribute set of anything` | The addon configures in `conf` folder in ini format with global sections. Each item is written to the corresponding file. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.settings.globalOptions` | `open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))` | The global options in `config` file in ini format. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.settings.inputMethod` | `open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))` | The input method configure in `profile` file in ini format. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.waylandFrontend` | `boolean` | Use the Wayland input method frontend. See [Using Fcitx 5 on Wayland](https://fcitx-im.org/wiki/Using_Fcitx_5_on_Wayland). |
| `i18n.inputMethod.ibus.engines` | `list of ibus-engine` | Enabled IBus engines. Available engines are: `anthy`, `bamboo`, `cangjie`, `chewing`, `hangul`, `libpinyin`, `libthai`, `m17n`, `mozc`, `mozc-ut`, `pinyin`, `rime`, `table`, `table-chinese`, `table-others`, `typing-booster`, `typing-booster-unwrapped`, `uniemoji`. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.ibus.panel` | `null or absolute path` | Replace the IBus panel with another panel. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.ibus.waylandFrontend` | `boolean` | Use the Wayland input method frontend. This doesn't set `GTK_IM_MODULE` and `QT_IM_MODULE` environment variables. See [Using Fcitx 5 on Wayland](https://fcitx-im.org/wiki/Using_Fcitx_5_on_Wayland#GTK_IM_MODULE). |
| `i18n.inputMethod.kime.daemonModules` | `list of (one of "Xim", "Wayland", "Indicator")` | List of enabled daemon modules |
| `i18n.inputMethod.kime.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | extra kime configuration. Refer to <https://github.com/Riey/kime/blob/v3.1.1/docs/CONFIGURATION.md> for details on supported values. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.kime.iconColor` | `one of "Black", "White"` | Color of the indicator icon |
| `i18n.inputMethod.type` | `null or one of "ibus", "fcitx5", "nabi", "uim", "hime", "kime"` | Select the enabled input method. Input methods is a software to input symbols that are not available on standard input devices. Input methods are specially used to input Chinese, Japanese and Korean characters. Currently the following input methods are available in NixOS: - ibus: The intelligent input bus, extra input engines can be added using `i18n.inputMethod.ibus.engines`. - fcitx5: The next generation of fcitx, addons (including engines, dictionaries, skins) can be added using `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.addons`. - nabi: A Korean input method based on XIM. Nabi doesn't support Qt 5. - uim: The universal input method, is a library with a XIM bridge. uim mainly support Chinese, Japanese and Korean. - hime: An extremely easy-to-use input method framework. - kime: Koream IME. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.uim.toolbar` | `one of "gtk", "gtk3", "gtk-systray", "gtk3-systray", "qt5"` | selected UIM toolbar. |
| `i18n.localeCharsets` | `attribute set of string` | Per each {option}`i18n.extraLocaleSettings`, choose the character set to use for it. Essentially defaults to UTF-8 for all of them. Note that for a locale category that uses the `C` locale, setting a character set to it via this setting is ignored. |
